prompt: I just set up the MongoDB MCP server but I don't see any atlas-streams tools in my Claude session. I need to create a connection to my Kafka cluster and deploy a processor. What should I do?

assertions.name: diagnoses_preview_features
assertions.description: Identifies previewFeatures: ["streams"] as the likely missing MCP server config
assertions.reason: Atlas Stream Processing tools are a preview feature and require explicit opt-in in the MCP server config

assertions.name: shows_config_fix
assertions.description: Shows the specific JSON config change to add previewFeatures: ["streams"]

assertions.name: offers_cli_interim
assertions.description: Offers Atlas CLI as an interim option while the user fixes the MCP config

assertions.name: no_false_tool_call
assertions.description: Does NOT attempt to call atlas-streams-* tools as if they exist

config_fix.description: Add previewFeatures to the MCP server configuration
config_fix.after_restart: Restart Claude (start a new session) to load the updated config

cli_interim.description: While fixing the MCP config, use Atlas CLI for immediate needs
cli_interim.create_workspace: atlas streams instances create <name> --provider AWS --region VIRGINIA_USA --projectId <id>
cli_interim.reference: See references/atlas-cli-streams.md for full CLI command reference

fallback_priority: 1. Fix previewFeatures config and restart Claude (preferred — enables all MCP tool features)
2. Use Atlas CLI for CRUD operations (interim — lacks diagnose, safety checks, elicitation)
3. Use sp.process() in mongosh for pipeline testing (always available)

common_mistakes.mistake: Generic 'check your config' advice without identifying previewFeatures
common_mistakes.error: User cannot fix the issue without knowing the specific setting
common_mistakes.fix: Specifically identify previewFeatures: ['streams'] as the missing config key

skill_reference: SKILL.md lines 21-27: 'If MCP tools are unavailable: 1. Verify previewFeatures: ["streams"] enabled 2. Use atlas streams CLI 3. Use sp.process() for prototyping'
